title: Scale CloudNative-PG PostgreSQL to 3-node HA cluster with connection pooling and automated backups
subtasks: Update CloudNative-PG Cluster CR to set instances: 3 (1 primary, 2 replicas) for HA.
Configure synchronous replication if required for data durability, otherwise use async for performance.
Enable PgBouncer connection pooling via the CloudNative-PG PgBouncer integration or a separate deployment; update service endpoints and ConfigMap references.
Set up automated daily base backups and continuous WAL archiving to S3/R2 (configure backup section in Cluster CR with S3 endpoint, credentials, and retention policy).
Test failover by promoting a replica and verifying application reconnection.
Document and test backup/restore procedures.

title: Scale Redis/Valkey to HA with Sentinel and configure maxmemory policy
subtasks: Update Redis/Valkey CR or Helm values to deploy 1 primary and 2 replicas, plus 3 Sentinel pods for HA.
Configure PodDisruptionBudgets and anti-affinity to ensure quorum and spread across nodes.
Set a maxmemory policy (e.g., allkeys-lru) and appropriate memory limits for production workloads.
Update the ConfigMap and service endpoints to point to the Sentinel-managed endpoint.
Test failover by killing the primary and verifying Sentinel promotes a replica.
Document Redis failover and recovery procedures.

title: Configure S3/R2 object storage for production with CDN and erasure coding
subtasks: Enable erasure coding or equivalent data durability features on Cloudflare R2 buckets for product images and event photos.
Review and update bucket CORS and lifecycle policies for production (e.g., retention, versioning, public access).
Integrate Cloudflare CDN for S3/R2 buckets to serve static assets with edge caching.
Test upload and retrieval of large files and verify CDN cache hit via response headers.
Document S3/R2 backup and recovery procedures.

title: Configure TLS certificates and Ingress for all public endpoints
subtasks: Install and configure cert-manager with Let's Encrypt or internal CA for automatic TLS certificate issuance.
Create Ingress resources for web frontend, Morgan agent, and any other public APIs, referencing the appropriate TLS secrets.
Configure Ingress controller (NGINX or Envoy) with secure defaults: HTTP/2, HSTS, redirect HTTP to HTTPS.
Integrate with Cloudflare Tunnel for external access, ensuring Ingress is only accessible via the tunnel.
Test certificate issuance, renewal, and HTTPS access for all endpoints.

title: Configure Cloudflare CDN and WAF for static assets and web frontend
subtasks: Set up Cloudflare DNS and CDN for the main domain and subdomains (e.g., www, morgan).
Enable SSL/TLS termination at Cloudflare edge with 'Full (Strict)' mode.
Configure cache rules for static asset paths and enable Auto Minify for JS/CSS/HTML.
Enable Cloudflare WAF with recommended rules for OWASP Top 10 and bot mitigation.
Test cache hit/miss and WAF rule enforcement from external networks.

title: Apply Kubernetes NetworkPolicies to restrict pod-to-pod traffic
subtasks: Map all required service-to-service communication flows (e.g., backend → PostgreSQL, backend → Redis, Morgan → backend, cloudflared → web frontend).
Create a default-deny ingress NetworkPolicy for each namespace.
Define explicit allow rules for each required flow, using podSelectors and namespaceSelectors.
Test connectivity from authorized pods and verify unauthorized connections are blocked.
Document the network policy matrix and update as new services are added.

title: Configure observability: OpenTelemetry Collector, Jaeger with persistent storage, and SLO alerting
subtasks: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector as a DaemonSet or Deployment, configure to collect traces, metrics, and logs from all services.
Set appropriate sampling rates for traces (e.g., 10% for high-volume endpoints, 100% for errors).
Deploy Jaeger with persistent storage (e.g., backed by PostgreSQL or Elasticsearch) to retain traces for at least 30 days.
Integrate application services with OpenTelemetry SDKs and verify traces are ingested.
Define and apply Prometheus alert rules for SLO violations (e.g., error rate, latency, availability).
Test alerting by simulating SLO breaches and verifying alert delivery.
